message:
chore(beads): sync tracker state for bd-1xwvk.1 and bd-2f7uj

Update beads issue tracker reflecting concurrent agent activity:
- bd-1xwvk.1 (Separation Logic specifications): status updated to in_progress,
  assigned to WhiteCreek
- bd-2f7uj (Migration Strategy + Rollback Plan): notes updated with DustyStream
  draft plan covering layout toggle, A/B profiling, rollback steps, user impact,
  validation checklist, and determinism guard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1xwvk","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Separation Logic Proofs for Obligation Safety","description":"Asupersync's obligation system manages heap resources (channels, permits, leases) that must satisfy ownership invariants. Separation Logic (§5.8) provides the formal framework to prove these invariants — specifically that obligations are never duplicated, never leaked, and always released. This addresses the verification gap: we have runtime checks but no formal proofs. Implementation approach: Use Creusot or Verus for Rust-native deductive verification of obligation invariants. Start with the simplest obligation (SendPermit) and prove: (1) No aliasing — exactly one owner at any time, (2) No leak — every allocated permit is eventually released, (3) No use-after-release — released permits cannot be used. This is Tier C on the Formal Assurance Ladder (§0.9), required for safety-critical primitives.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:15.553128728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:16.304639644Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","separation-logic","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk","depends_on_id":"bd-3u5d3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1xwvk.1","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Write Separation Logic specifications for obligation invariants","description":"Write formal Separation Logic specifications for all obligation invariants in Asupersync. For each obligation type (SendPermit, Ack, Lease, Release, Reserve, Commit), define: (1) The resource predicate -- what heap state the obligation owns, (2) The frame condition -- what it does NOT touch, (3) Pre/post conditions for creation, transfer, and release, (4) The separating conjunction properties preventing aliasing. Use Iris-style notation compatible with Creusot/Verus. Must handle concurrent access (concurrent separation logic with lock invariants), cancellation, and nesting. Deliverable: specification document with formal predicates. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: (Specification deliverable -- define the verification logging contract.) INFO: specification written for obligation type {type}: predicates={n}, pre_post_pairs={n}. DEBUG: resource predicate detail: obligation={type}, predicate={formal_notation}. WARN: specification gap identified: obligation {type} has uncovered operation {op_name}. Metrics (for proof verification, defined here for consistency): proof_verification_time_ms (histogram by obligation_type), proof_axiom_count (gauge by obligation_type), proof_admitted_count (gauge, target: 0), spec_coverage_ratio (gauge -- fraction of obligation API methods with formal specs).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:24.219448951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:14.755825795Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","separation-logic","specification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

For each obligation type (SendPermit, Ack, Lease, Release, Reserve, Commit), define: (1) The resource predicate -- what heap state the obligation owns, (2) The frame condition -- what it does NOT touch, (3) Pre/post conditions for creation, transfer, and release, (4) The separating conjunction properties preventing aliasing. Use Iris-style notation compatible with Creusot/Verus. Must handle concurrent access (concurrent separation logic with lock invariants), cancellation, and nesting. Metrics (for proof verification, defined here for consistency): proof_verification_time_ms (histogram by obligation_type), proof_axiom_count (gauge by obligation_type), proof_admitted_count (gauge, target: 0), spec_coverage_ratio (gauge -- fraction of obligation API methods with formal specs).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteCreek","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:24.219448951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T03:54:00.976951166Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","separation-logic","specification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xwvk.2","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Prove SendPermit no-aliasing invariant","description":"Prove using Separation Logic that the SendPermit obligation satisfies the no-aliasing invariant: at any program point, exactly one task owns any given SendPermit.

BACKGROUND (Separation Logic, Graveyard section 5.8): Separation logic extends Hoare logic with the separating conjunction (P * Q), meaning P and Q hold on disjoint portions of the heap. This makes it possible to reason about ownership: if SendPermit(p) * Frame, then the frame (rest of the heap) does not contain p. Transfer of ownership is modeled by consuming the resource predicate from the caller and giving it to the callee. This is the formal foundation for Rust's ownership model.

PROOF STRUCTURE: (1) Allocation proof: show that SendPermit::new() creates a fresh resource predicate SendPermit(p) that is not aliased. Formally: { emp } let p = SendPermit::new() { SendPermit(p) }. The emp (empty heap) precondition ensures no prior alias exists. (2) Transfer proof: show that SendPermit::transfer(from, to) consumes the caller's predicate and gives it to the callee. Formally: { SendPermit(p) @ from } transfer(p, to) { SendPermit(p) @ to }. The separating conjunction ensures no duplication -- the predicate moves, it is not copied. (3) Release proof: show that SendPermit::release() consumes the predicate, returning to empty heap. Formally: { SendPermit(p) } release(p) { emp }. After release, no reference to p exists.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Use Creusot (preferred) or Verus for Rust-native deductive verification. Creusot provides a Rust-embedded proof language compatible with separation logic. (2) Annotate the actual SendPermit implementation with pre/post conditions using #[requires] and #[ensures] attributes. (3) Ghost state: define a ghost map of permit ownership: ghost_permits: GhostMap<PermitId, TaskId>. Invariant: for any permit p, ghost_permits.get(p) returns exactly one task. (4) Handle concurrent creation: different permits are independent (separating conjunction of distinct SendPermit(p1) * SendPermit(p2)). (5) Handle task cancellation: the Drop impl for SendPermit must consume the predicate (release on drop). (6) Integration with channel state machine: the channel's internal state must be consistent with the outstanding permits.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Creusot or Verus verifier accepts the proof without admits/sorry/trusted blocks. (2) Proof covers all public API methods of SendPermit (new, transfer, release, drop). (3) Proof handles concurrent creation of independent permits. (4) Proof handles the cancellation path (Drop impl). (5) Proof assumptions are documented and justified (no hidden axioms). (6) Proof can be re-verified by CI (automated verification as part of build).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Proof verification results logged to CI: proof_name, verifier (creusot|verus), verification_time_ms, result (verified|failed|timeout), admitted_count (must be 0), axiom_count, lemma_count. EvidenceLedger entry: component=separation_logic_proof, action=sendpermit_no_aliasing_verified, includes proof_size_loc, assumption_count.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) The proof itself is the test -- verification by the tool is the acceptance criterion. (2) Additionally, write runtime tests that exercise the same API paths and verify the invariants dynamically (defense in depth). (3) Mutation testing: introduce known bugs in SendPermit (e.g., allow cloning), verify the proof fails. (4) If using Creusot: cargo creusot in CI as a required check.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:32.110014843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:32:46.977945166Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","proof","separation-logic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:18Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:18Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xwvk.3","title":"[ALIEN-GRAVEYARD] Prove obligation no-leak invariant","description":"Prove using Separation Logic that obligations are never leaked -- every allocated obligation is eventually released. This is a liveness property complementing the safety properties proved in bd-1xwvk.2.

BACKGROUND (Separation Logic Liveness, Graveyard section 5.8): While safety properties (no aliasing, no use-after-free) can be proved with standard separation logic, liveness properties (every resource is eventually freed) require additional reasoning about termination and progress. The approach combines separation logic resource predicates with a ghost counter argument: define a ghost counter for outstanding obligations and show it monotonically decreases on every execution path until it reaches zero.

PROOF STRUCTURE: (1) Define ghost counter: ghost obligation_count: GhostNat. Invariant: obligation_count == number of outstanding (unreleased) obligations in the heap. (2) Allocation increases counter: { obligation_count == n } let o = Obligation::new() { obligation_count == n + 1 * Obligation(o) }. (3) Release decreases counter: { obligation_count == n * Obligation(o) } o.release() { obligation_count == n - 1 }. (4) Task completion releases all: at task exit, all obligations held by the task must be released (enforced by Drop impls). Show: after task.await completes, obligation_count for that task == 0. (5) Cancellation path: use the severity lattice (Ok < Err < Cancelled < Panicked) to show that higher-severity outcomes still release obligations. Specifically: the Drop impl runs regardless of severity (Rust guarantees Drop on unwind). (6) Region closure (structured concurrency): when a region quiesces (all child tasks complete), all obligations held by any child are released. Show: after region.quiesce(), sum of obligation_count for all children == 0.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Use Creusot or Verus with ghost state for the obligation counter. (2) Key insight: Rusts ownership + Drop guarantee is the foundation -- every value is either moved or dropped, so the proof can rely on the Drop impl being called. (3) The proof must handle: (a) Normal return path -- obligation released explicitly before task returns. (b) Error path -- obligation released in error handler or by Drop. (c) Panic path -- Drop runs during stack unwinding. (d) Cancel path -- Asupersync cancellation triggers task abort, which runs Drop. (4) Challenge: Leaking via mem::forget or Rc cycles. Document these as proof assumptions (Asupersync does not use mem::forget on obligations; Rc cycles are prevented by the DAG task structure).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Verifier accepts the proof for all four paths (normal, error, panic, cancel). (2) Ghost obligation counter reaches zero after task/region completion. (3) Proof assumptions are explicitly documented (no mem::forget, no Rc cycles for obligations). (4) Proof handles nested obligations (obligations within obligations). (5) No admits/sorry/trusted blocks. (6) CI-verifiable.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Proof verification results logged to CI: proof_name, verifier, verification_time_ms, result, path_coverage (normal|error|panic|cancel), admitted_count (must be 0). EvidenceLedger entry: component=separation_logic_proof, action=obligation_no_leak_verified, includes paths_proved, assumption_count, ghost_counter_max_observed. Runtime defense-in-depth: add debug_assert!(obligation_count == 0) at region quiescence points, logged to EvidenceLedger.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Formal verification is the primary test. (2) Runtime defense: debug-mode obligation counter that panics on leak (task completes with outstanding obligations). (3) Stress test: 10M random task trees (from bd-2ktrc.7) with obligation tracking -- verify zero leaks. (4) Mutation testing: introduce a code path that skips Drop (e.g., conditional mem::forget), verify the proof breaks.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:17:39.841277806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:33:13.927733486Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","liveness","proof","separation-logic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xwvk.4","title":"Unit and integration tests for Separation Logic specifications and proofs","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the Separation Logic proof infrastructure (specifications in bd-1xwvk.1, no-aliasing proof in bd-1xwvk.2, no-leak proof in bd-1xwvk.3).

BACKGROUND: While the formal proofs are verified by the proof tool (Creusot/Verus), we need a complementary runtime test suite that: (1) validates the proof assumptions hold in practice, (2) provides defense-in-depth with dynamic checking, (3) catches regressions when the implementation changes, and (4) verifies the proof tool integration with CI.

UNIT TESTS:
(1) Proof tool integration: verify cargo creusot (or verus) runs successfully on all annotated files and produces a verified result. This is a CI gate -- any proof regression fails the build.
(2) Ghost state consistency: add debug-mode runtime checks that mirror the ghost state in proofs. For each obligation lifecycle (create, transfer, release), verify the runtime ghost counter matches expected values.
(3) Specification coverage: for each formal specification in bd-1xwvk.1, write a runtime test that exercises the specified behavior and verifies the pre/post conditions hold dynamically.
(4) No-aliasing dynamic check: use a global debug-mode registry of outstanding permits. On create, insert. On transfer, move entry. On release, remove. At any point, assert each permit has exactly one owner. Test with 100 concurrent permit lifecycles.
(5) No-leak dynamic check: at region quiescence, assert the obligation counter for that region is zero. Test with 50 regions, each spawning 10 tasks with random obligations.
(6) Cancellation path coverage: verify Drop runs on all cancellation severities (Ok, Err, Cancelled, Panicked) and releases obligations correctly.
(7) Mutation testing: introduce known-bad mutations (clone a permit, skip release on one path) and verify either the proof tool rejects or the runtime check catches the violation.

INTEGRATION TESTS:
(8) Full obligation lifecycle under FrankenLab: run all obligation types through complete lifecycles with deterministic scheduling. Verify ghost counters are zero at completion.
(9) Cross-obligation interaction: test scenarios where multiple obligation types interact (SendPermit held while Lease is active), verify no cross-contamination.

PROPERTY-BASED TESTS (proptest):
(10) Random obligation lifecycle sequences: generate random create/transfer/release sequences, verify invariants (no aliasing, no leak) hold for all valid sequences.
(11) Random cancellation injection: generate random task trees with random cancel points, verify all obligations released after quiescence.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each test logs: test_name, proof_tool_used, verification_time_ms, ghost_counter_final, obligations_created, obligations_released, obligations_leaked (must be 0). On ghost counter mismatch: log full obligation lifecycle trace with create/transfer/release events. CI summary: proofs_verified, runtime_checks_passed, mutations_caught.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) All formal proofs verify successfully in CI. (2) Runtime ghost counter checks pass for 10000 random obligation lifecycles. (3) All mutation tests detect the injected bug (either proof tool or runtime check). (4) Zero obligation leaks across all cancellation scenarios. (5) Tests complete in under 5 minutes on CI.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:31.559750702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:58.181455617Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","separation-logic","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xwvk.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1xwvk.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
@@ -2166,7 +2166,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2eyv","title":"Coverage metrics: track exploration completeness","description":"# Coverage Metrics Implementation

## Goal

Implement comprehensive coverage metrics for schedule exploration, enabling users to understand how thoroughly their concurrent code has been tested.

## Background

Traditional code coverage (lines, branches) doesn't capture concurrency:
- 100% line coverage can miss race conditions
- Need metrics for scheduling decisions, interleavings, states

## Coverage Dimensions

### Schedule Coverage
```rust
pub struct ScheduleCoverage {
    /// Total schedules explored
    schedules_explored: usize,
    
    /// Distinct schedules (after normalization)
    distinct_schedules: usize,
    
    /// Estimated total schedules (if computable)
    estimated_total: Option<usize>,
    
    /// Coverage percentage (if total known)
    coverage_percent: Option<f64>,
}
```

### State Coverage
```rust
pub struct StateCoverage {
    /// Distinct states visited
    states_visited: HashSet<StateHash>,
    
    /// State space estimate
    estimated_states: Option<usize>,
    
    /// States per scheduling point
    states_per_point: HashMap<SchedulePoint, usize>,
}
```

### Race Coverage
```rust
pub struct RaceCoverage {
    /// All detected races
    races_detected: HashSet<Race>,
    
    /// Races explored in both orders
    races_covered: HashSet<Race>,
    
    /// Races explored in only one order
    races_partial: HashSet<Race>,
}
```

### Transition Coverage
```rust
pub struct TransitionCoverage {
    /// All state transitions observed
    transitions: HashMap<(StateHash, EventKind), StateHash>,
    
    /// Transition pairs (s1->s2, s2->s3 seen)
    transition_pairs: HashSet<(StateHash, StateHash, StateHash)>,
}
```

## CoverageMetrics

```rust
pub struct CoverageMetrics {
    schedule: ScheduleCoverage,
    state: StateCoverage,
    race: RaceCoverage,
    transition: TransitionCoverage,
    
    /// Time spent exploring
    exploration_time: Duration,
    
    /// Bugs found
    bugs_found: usize,
}

impl CoverageMetrics {
    /// Record a new schedule explored
    fn record_schedule(&mut self, schedule: &NormalizedTrace);
    
    /// Record state visited
    fn record_state(&mut self, state: StateHash);
    
    /// Record race covered
    fn record_race(&mut self, race: &Race, order: RaceOrder);
    
    /// Generate coverage report
    fn report(&self) -> CoverageReport;
}
```

## Coverage Report

```rust
pub struct CoverageReport {
    /// Summary statistics
    summary: CoverageSummary,
    
    /// Uncovered items (if identifiable)
    uncovered: UncoveredItems,
    
    /// Recommendations for more coverage
    recommendations: Vec<Recommendation>,
}

pub struct CoverageSummary {
    schedules: String,      // '1,234 / ~10,000 (12.3%)'
    states: String,         // '567 distinct states'
    races: String,          // '23 races, 20 fully covered'
    transitions: String,    // '456 unique transitions'
    time: String,           // '2m 34s'
}

pub enum Recommendation {
    /// Need more exploration time
    MoreTime { estimated_remaining: Duration },
    
    /// Specific race needs other order
    ExploreRace { race: Race, missing_order: RaceOrder },
    
    /// Unexplored state region
    UnexploredRegion { hint: String },
}
```

## Progress Tracking

```rust
pub trait CoverageProgress {
    /// Called periodically during exploration
    fn on_progress(&mut self, metrics: &CoverageMetrics);
    
    /// Called when new coverage achieved
    fn on_new_coverage(&mut self, kind: CoverageKind, details: &str);
}

pub struct ProgressPrinter;
impl CoverageProgress for ProgressPrinter {
    fn on_progress(&mut self, metrics: &CoverageMetrics) {
        eprintln!(
            'Explored {} schedules, {} states, {} races covered',
            metrics.schedule.schedules_explored,
            metrics.state.states_visited.len(),
            metrics.race.races_covered.len(),
        );
    }
}
```

## Convergence Detection

Detect when exploration is 'done enough':
```rust
pub struct ConvergenceDetector {
    /// Window of recent exploration
    recent_states: VecDeque<usize>,
    
    /// Threshold for convergence
    threshold: ConvergenceThreshold,
}

pub struct ConvergenceThreshold {
    /// Stop if no new states in N schedules
    no_new_states_for: usize,
    
    /// Stop if coverage rate drops below X%
    min_coverage_rate: f64,
    
    /// Always run at least N schedules
    min_schedules: usize,
}

impl ConvergenceDetector {
    fn check_convergence(&mut self, metrics: &CoverageMetrics) -> bool {
        // No new states in last N schedules?
        // Coverage rate below threshold?
    }
}
```

## State Fingerprinting

Efficient state comparison:
```rust
pub type StateHash = u64;

pub trait StateFingerprint {
    fn fingerprint(&self) -> StateHash;
}

impl StateFingerprint for RuntimeState {
    fn fingerprint(&self) -> StateHash {
        // Hash relevant state components
        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();
        self.tasks.hash(&mut hasher);
        self.obligations.hash(&mut hasher);
        // ... etc
        hasher.finish()
    }
}
```

## Integration

```rust
impl ScheduleExplorer {
    /// Run with coverage tracking
    fn explore_with_coverage<F>(&mut self, program: F) -> (ExplorationResult, CoverageReport);
    
    /// Get current coverage
    fn coverage(&self) -> &CoverageMetrics;
    
    /// Check if converged
    fn is_converged(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Testing

- Coverage increases with exploration
- Convergence detection works
- State fingerprinting is deterministic
- Report generation
- Progress callbacks

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ScheduleCoverage metrics
- [ ] StateCoverage with fingerprinting
- [ ] RaceCoverage with order tracking
- [ ] TransitionCoverage
- [ ] CoverageReport generation
- [ ] Progress callbacks
- [ ] Convergence detection
- [ ] Integration with ScheduleExplorer
- [ ] Human-readable reports","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:29:07.234634853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:26.793477876Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:42:26.793403267Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","dpor","phase5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2eyv","depends_on_id":"bd-2pct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:48Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2eyv","depends_on_id":"bd-yt6g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2f4o","title":"HTTP/1 server (listener + graceful shutdown)","description":"Goal: HTTP/1.1 server built on connection lifecycle + body streaming. Includes listener accept loop, graceful shutdown, connection limits, and structured cancellation propagation. Must avoid stdout and surface observability via tracing.  {"id":"bd-2f7","title":"[Net] Implement UdpSocket","description":"# UDP Socket Implementation

## Overview
Full UDP networking with datagram-oriented operations.

## UdpSocket

```rust
pub struct UdpSocket {
    inner: sys::UdpSocket,
}

impl UdpSocket {
    /// Bind to address
    pub async fn bind(addr: impl ToSocketAddrs) -> io::Result<Self>;
    
    /// Connect to remote (for send/recv)
    pub async fn connect(&self, addr: impl ToSocketAddrs) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Send datagram to specific address
    pub async fn send_to(&self, buf: &[u8], target: impl ToSocketAddrs) -> io::Result<usize>;
    
    /// Receive datagram with source address
    pub async fn recv_from(&self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> io::Result<(usize, SocketAddr)>;
    
    /// Send to connected address
    pub async fn send(&self, buf: &[u8]) -> io::Result<usize>;
    
    /// Receive from connected address
    pub async fn recv(&self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> io::Result<usize>;
    
    /// Peek at datagram without consuming
    pub async fn peek_from(&self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> io::Result<(usize, SocketAddr)>;
    
    /// Get local address
    pub fn local_addr(&self) -> io::Result<SocketAddr>;
    
    /// Get peer address (if connected)
    pub fn peer_addr(&self) -> io::Result<SocketAddr>;
    
    /// Set broadcast
    pub fn set_broadcast(&self, on: bool) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Set multicast loopback
    pub fn set_multicast_loop_v4(&self, on: bool) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Join multicast group
    pub fn join_multicast_v4(&self, multiaddr: Ipv4Addr, interface: Ipv4Addr) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Leave multicast group
    pub fn leave_multicast_v4(&self, multiaddr: Ipv4Addr, interface: Ipv4Addr) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Set TTL
    pub fn set_ttl(&self, ttl: u32) -> io::Result<()>;
}
```

## Datagram Streams

```rust
/// Datagram receive stream
pub struct RecvStream<'a> {
    socket: &'a UdpSocket,
    buf_size: usize,
}

impl Stream for RecvStream<'_> {
    type Item = io::Result<(Vec<u8>, SocketAddr)>;
}

/// Datagram send sink
pub struct SendSink<'a> {
    socket: &'a UdpSocket,
}

impl Sink<(Vec<u8>, SocketAddr)> for SendSink<'_> {
    type Error = io::Error;
}
```

## Cancel-Safety
- send_to/send: atomic datagram, cancel-safe
- recv_from/recv: cancel = datagram discarded (UDP is unreliable anyway)
- connect: cancel-safe (stateless)

## Multicast Support
```rust
impl UdpSocket {
    /// Join multicast group (IPv6)
    pub fn join_multicast_v6(&self, multiaddr: &Ipv6Addr, interface: u32) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Leave multicast group (IPv6)
    pub fn leave_multicast_v6(&self, multiaddr: &Ipv6Addr, interface: u32) -> io::Result<()>;
    
    /// Set multicast TTL
    pub fn set_multicast_ttl_v4(&self, ttl: u32) -> io::Result<()>;
}
```

## Testing
- bind and send_to/recv_from
- connected mode
- multicast join/leave
- broadcast
- concurrent send/recv
- large datagrams

## Files
- src/net/udp.rs

-{"id":"bd-2f7uj","title":"[bd-2ijqf] Migration Strategy + Rollback Plan","description":"Goal: define a safe migration/rollback plan for introducing sharded tables with minimal user-facing risk.

Scope / Deliverables:
- Decide whether to gate sharding behind a feature flag or leave always-on.
- If gated: add a single switch to select old vs new locking layout for A/B profiling.
- Document rollback steps and known risks (deadlocks, determinism regressions).
- Document user impact: any config or env changes, expected performance changes, and how to enable/disable metrics.
- Provide a validation checklist: unit tests, lab tests, and E2E harness (bd-ecaeo) to confirm correctness after flip.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Plan documented in bead notes and reflected in code comments where applicable.
- No behavior changes when sharding is disabled (if using a flag).
- Clear guidance for users on how to reproduce baseline vs sharded runs and how to roll back safely.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Plan documented in bead notes and reflected in code comments where applicable.
2. No behavior changes when sharding is disabled (if using a flag); provide explicit toggles and defaults.
3. Validation checklist includes unit + lab + E2E (bd-ecaeo) commands and expected artifacts with structured logs.
4. Clear guidance for users on how to reproduce baseline vs sharded runs and how to roll back safely.","notes":"DustyStream draft plan (migration + rollback):

1) Layout toggle: add internal StateLayout enum {Unified, Sharded} behind RuntimeBuilder config (e.g., RuntimeBuilder::state_layout(StateLayout::Sharded)). Keep default Unified until sharded validation passes, then flip default to Sharded while keeping Unified available for rollback/A-B. Avoid public API forks; keep callsites unified. Optional cargo feature 'state-sharding' can compile out Sharded path only if it truly removes locks/fields; otherwise prefer runtime flag only.

2) A/B profiling: document explicit run recipes for Unified vs Sharded with same seed/config. If lock metrics are enabled (feature 'lock-metrics' per spec), capture per-shard wait/hold stats. Store artifacts under target/ (or existing test artifact path). No behavioral shims.

3) Rollback steps: switch layout to Unified (or disable state-sharding feature), re-run contention harness and lab determinism suite with same seed, and compare trace + metrics artifacts. If divergence, keep Unified default until resolved.

4) User impact: no API changes; only new RuntimeBuilder option + optional env override (if added). Document deterministic seed usage and any new feature flags (lock-metrics) required for comparisons.

5) Validation checklist: fmt/check/clippy; unit + lab tests; E2E contention harness (bd-ecaeo); trace replay/normalization equivalence between Unified and Sharded for identical seed/config; verify cancel/obligation/region-close invariants.

6) Code comment placement (when implementing): RuntimeBuilder::state_layout docs + RuntimeConfig struct comment (layout/feature flags + rollback note).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyStream","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:00:08.255483077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:24:32.554515666Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2f7uj","depends_on_id":"bd-1tc1m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2f7uj","depends_on_id":"bd-2ijqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:23Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1044,"issue_id":"bd-2f7uj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Migration/Rollback plan (draft):

1) **Layout toggle**: introduce internal `RuntimeStateLayout` enum (Unified vs Sharded) behind a single config switch (e.g., `RuntimeBuilder::state_layout(StateLayout::Sharded)`), defaulting to Sharded after validation. Avoid public API forks; keep callsites unified. If we add a Cargo feature (e.g., `state-sharding`), it should only *disable* the sharded layout and compile out extra tables/locks; no behavioral shims.

2) **A/B profiling**: keep the unified layout accessible for targeted benchmarking only. Make the switch explicit in docs and tests to avoid ambiguity.

3) **Rollback steps**: if regressions occur, flip layout to Unified in config (or disable feature), re-run baseline harness `tests/contention_e2e.rs` with same seed, and compare artifacts.

4) **User impact**: no API changes; only runtime configuration gains an optional layout flag and lock-metrics feature. Document environment variables/flags, deterministic seeds, and artifact locations.

5) **Validation checklist**: fmt/check/clippy, unit+lab tests, E2E harness (`--features lock-metrics`), trace replay/normalization checks, and comparison against baseline artifacts.

6) **Determinism guard**: require trace equivalence or acceptable normalized replay equivalence between Unified and Sharded layouts for the same seed/config.","created_at":"2026-02-05T01:35:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2fh3z","title":"[SPORK][GenServer] Core Trait + Runtime Loop","description":"Implement the core Spork GenServer abstraction on top of `src/actor.rs` and cancel-correct channel/session primitives.

Includes:
- typed state
- `init` / `terminate`
- `handle_call` / `handle_cast` / `handle_info`
- system messages: Down/Exit, timeout ticks

Constraints:
- region-owned, cancellation-aware, deterministic under lab.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:
- Trait + runtime loop exist and compile in core crate.
- call/cast/info paths have no obligation leaks in lab tests.
- Server stop releases any registry leases and finalizes correctly.","notes":"GenServer core continues in src/gen_server.rs (exported in src/lib.rs).

Major invariant fix:
- GenServer call replies are now true linear obligations: call enqueues a reserved tracked oneshot permit and server receives a Reply wrapping it. Dropping a Reply without send/abort now panics (obligation token leak) as intended.
- On enqueue failure, the reserved reply permit is explicitly aborted to avoid leaking.
- call/cast/try_cast reject new messages once server state is Stopping/Stopped (prevents churn during drain).

Tests:
- Added gen_server_spawn_and_call (deterministic lab test) to cover call path.

Quality gates (local, isolated target dirs):
- cargo fmt --check: OK
- cargo check --all-targets: OK
- cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings: OK
- cargo test gen_server: OK (9 tests)

Coordination:
- Not running full cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings here; historically blocked by other beads touching src/supervision.rs tests.
- I made small clippy unblock fixes in src/monitor.rs and src/supervision.rs while running gates; see Agent Mail for details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"QuietStream","created_at":"2026-02-06T02:59:17.565299957Z","created_by":"NavyBeacon","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:34:55.898982598Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:34:55.898915934Z","close_reason":"GenServer core trait and runtime loop fully implemented in src/gen_server.rs (3000+ lines). Includes typed state, init/terminate, handle_call/handle_cast/handle_info, system messages, region-owned, cancellation-aware, deterministic under lab. 33+ existing tests pass. Code was implemented by previous agents but bead not closed.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["genserver","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fh3z","depends_on_id":"bd-1xdfe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fh3z","depends_on_id":"bd-1zok9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fnh","title":"bd-ut05: stream::merge advanced scenarios","description":"## Unit Tests for src/stream/merge.rs (288 LOC, 6 existing tests)

Existing tests cover basic 2-stream merge. This bead targets advanced scenarios:

### New Test Cases
- Merge 3+ streams: all items from all streams appear exactly once
- Merge with vastly unequal latencies (one fast, one slow) — fairness
- Merge where one stream errors mid-way — error propagation to merged output
- Merge where one stream is empty from start — other stream passes through
- Merge where both streams empty — immediate completion
- Merge cancellation: drop merged stream → verify both input streams cancelled
- Merge size_hint accuracy: sum of input hints
- Merge with streams that yield Ready then Pending alternately — interleaving correctness
- Merge under backpressure: consumer slower than producers

### Logging Requirements
Each test logs: items received in order, which source stream, total counts.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 9+ new tests in merge.rs #[cfg(test)]
- [ ] Fairness verified (neither stream starved)
- [ ] Cancellation propagation confirmed","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.537063319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.269733370Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:41:15.984497176Z","close_reason":"Completed","deleted_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.269720816Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","stream","unit-test"]}
